content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.9 `MemoryRun`
Document lines: 501-539

### 3.9 `MemoryRun`

Audit trail и debug слой.

Нужен, чтобы видеть:
- какой prompt сработал
- на какой модели
- что именно было retrieved
- какой budget был выделен
- какие ops были предложены
- какие ops реально применились
- где pipeline сломался

Рекомендуемые поля:
- `run_id`
- `namespace_id`
- `run_type: route | retrieve_update | canonicalize | construct_notes | reconcile_notes | extract_claims | reconcile_claims | update_profile | update_tasks | consolidate_notes | repair_memory | apply_retention | read_plan | compose_answer | compact`
- `trigger_mode: hot_path | background | manual | slow_path`
- `source_ids_json`
- `retrieved_item_ids_json`
- `retrieval_budget_json`
- `prompt_name`
- `prompt_version`
- `model_name`
- `input_hash`
- `output_json`
- `applied_ops_json`
- `repair_actions_json`
- `latency_ms`
- `token_input`
- `token_output`
- `status: success | failed | partial`
- `error_text`
- `created_at`

Инварианты:
- run log не должен исчезать silently
- `input_hash` полезен для идемпотентности и retry safety

        Candidate notes:
        [0] type=DOC_DIGEST title=MemoryRun as audit and debugging layer for pipeline observability summary=In section 3.9, `MemoryRun` is defined as an audit/debug layer to trace execution context (prompt, model, retrieval, budget, proposed vs applied ops) and diagnose pipeline failures, making runs inspectable end-to-end. scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.9 `MemoryRun` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md entities=entity:<0> keywords=MemoryRun,audit trail,debug layer,pipeline observability tags=data_model,memoryrun,doc_digest evidence=Audit trail и debug слой.
[1] type=DOC_DIGEST title=MemoryRun logging invariants and idempotency support summary=`MemoryRun` has strict operational invariants: run logs must not disappear silently, and `input_hash` is used to support idempotency and retry safety in pipeline execution. scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.9 `MemoryRun` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md entities=entity:<0> keywords=MemoryRun,invariants,input_hash,idempotency,retry safety tags=data_model,memoryrun,invariants evidence=Инварианты: - run log не должен исчезать silently - `input_hash` полезен для идемпотентности и retry safety

        Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Source component role and invariants in v3 data model summary=In section 3.1, `Source` is defined as the raw evidence layer that stores chat/tool/imported/external records for re-check and explainability; key invariants require immutable source content, separate `observed_at` from ingestion time, explicit delete policy, and `content_hash`-based dedup support. scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.1 `Source` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Source,evidence layer,observed_at,content_hash,delete policy tags=data_model,memory_architecture,source_invariants useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<1> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=v3 memory model layer decomposition and cross-cutting concerns summary=The v3 data model is intentionally split into layered components—Source (evidence), Entity/Claim (semantic graph), Note (intermediate context), Profile/Task/Episode (operational projections), and MemoryRun (audit)—to support typed memory, lineage, temporal truth, reconcilable updates, richer semantics, lifecycle/retention, and repairability. It also defines two cross-cutting concerns: scope and re...[truncated 18 chars] scope=Applies to 02. Data Model section defining v3 architecture in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=v3,data model layers,cross-cutting concerns,retrieval lifecycle tags=architecture,memory_model,doc_section useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<2> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Scope, truth/retrieval separation, and usage lifecycle fields in v3 summary=For most memory items except raw Source, v3 uses dual scope fields: human-readable scope_text and machine-readable scope_json (with scope_kind, IDs, environment, modality, audience) to support explainability, retrieval, and reconciliation. The model keeps truth status separate from retrieval_state, and uses lifecycle states (active/warm/cold/archived/deleted) plus usage fields (use_count, last_use...[truncated 61 chars] scope=Applies to cross-cutting field definitions in 02. Data Model section of agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=scope_text,scope_json,truth status,retrieval_state,use_count tags=cross_cutting,retrieval,lifecycle,schema useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<3> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Task memory is only for real future commitments summary=In 02. Data Model section 3.7, `Task` is defined as operational memory for future action and should be created only when there is a real commitment, not for plain questions or requests; each task must represent one clear obligation and be updated via status changes instead of duplicate task creation. scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.7 `Task` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Task,future action,commitment invariant,status update tags=data_model,task,invariants useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<4> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Note` is an intermediate semantic fragment for retrieval-ready but non-final knowledge summary=The `Note` component is positioned between raw source and final claims: it stores important contextual knowledge (e.g., decisions, rationale, plans, hypotheses, lessons, digests) that is useful for retrieval without hardening into immutable fact, with invariants enforcing one semantic fragment per note and self-contained meaning. scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.4 `Note` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Note,semantic fragment,retrieval,candidate_claims_json,invariants tags=data_model,note_component,memory_semantics useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<5> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Entity component as canonical memory anchor with reversible merge summary=In section 3.2, `Entity` is the canonical memory node that unifies mentions of the same object and anchors claims/tasks/notes/episodes; alias and merge mechanics are constrained so merge history is preserved, aliases point to the current canonical entity, and incorrect merges are reversible via audit trail. scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.2 `Entity` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Entity,canonical node,entity_aliases,merge,audit trail tags=data_model,entity_resolution,merge_invariants useCount=0

responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

structured.fullText: {"operations":[{"action":"insert","candidate_index":0,"links_to_create":[{"link_type":"refines","link_weight":0.96,"to_note_id":"hot-path:note:<1>"}],"patch":null,"reason":"New distinct note for section 3.9 detailing MemoryRun purpose/observability fields; not duplicated in existing notes.","target_note_id":null},{"action":"insert","candidate_index":1,"links_to_create":[{"link_type":"refines","link_weight":0.9,"to_note_id":"hot-path:note:<1>"}],"patch":null,"reason":"Captures specific MemoryRun invariants (non-disappearing run log, input_hash idempotency) not present as a standalone active note.","target_note_id":null}]}
